steps.title: Identify the critical requirement for an effective introduction
steps.content: To introduce a novel and author to an unfamiliar audience, you must convey both what the novel is about and why it deserves attention.

steps.title: Match the requirement to the best choice
steps.content: C succeeds by opening with the novel's defining features (much-praised, historical novel about colonial East Africa) and connecting this to the author's Nobel Prize, the most prestigious credential available. The other choices either describe the author without explaining the novel, emphasize reader praise over the author's highest credential, or lack credibility signals to establish importance. That matches choice C — C is correct.
